source: iConnections Global Alts 2025 — The Future of AI
summary: xAI has the highest return on capital in AI data centers among Western companies and will maintain this advantage
summary_extended: Baker and Gracias argue that xAI has the highest return on capital among Western AI data center operators, making it their highest-conviction AI infrastructure investment. They attribute this advantage to Elon Musk's capital efficiency philosophy, drawing a direct parallel to how Musk built the Tesla Gigafactory in China at dramatically lower cost than Western peers. They further argue that DeepSeek-style efficiency improvements in AI model training and inference disproportionately benefit high-ROC operators like xAI, since leaner compute economics amplify the advantage of those who already spend less per unit of output. xAI also benefits from a proprietary data moat via the X/Twitter platform, which provides unique training data unavailable to competitors. The investment implication is a direct long position in xAI, though xAI is currently private and has no US-listed equity ticker, limiting public market expression of this thesis.
detail: Both Baker and Gracias are invested in xAI. They argue xAI's data center return on capital is highest in the West, citing Elon Musk's capital efficiency philosophy (referencing Tesla China factory). DeepSeek-style efficiency improvements further advantage high-ROC operators. xAI also has proprietary data from X/Twitter platform.
confidence_evidence: Both speakers explicitly state they are invested in xAI, call it out by name multiple times, and frame it as their highest-conviction AI infrastructure bet with specific return-on-capital framing.
quote_fragment: xai has the highest Trend Capital certainly in the west
